result.summary: ClickHouse exposes a ClickHouse Cloud REST API, a database HTTP interface, an official MCP server implementation, and the `clickhousectl` CLI; Cloud API auth is documented, while the DB HTTP interface and CLI auth are only partially documented in the pages reviewed.
result.description: ClickHouse is a column-oriented database system for real-time analytics, available as open-source software and as the managed ClickHouse Cloud service. It is used to store and query analytical workloads, and also offers managed cloud administration features and AI-oriented tooling such as an MCP server.

clickhouse_cloud_api_key.label: ClickHouse Cloud API key and secret
clickhouse_cloud_api_key.setup: In the ClickHouse Cloud API docs, use the **OpenAPI** section (`/v1/openapi`) to manage API keys: list keys, create a key, view key details, update, or delete. The Cloud API docs state requests are authorized with `_basicAuth_`, so use the API key ID/username and secret/password from your ClickHouse Cloud organization when calling `https://api.clickhouse.cloud/v1`. Start from the Cloud API overview at [Cloud API overview](https://clickhouse.com/docs/cloud/manage/api/api-overview) or the API reference at [Swagger / ReDoc](https://clickhouse.com/docs/cloud/manage/api/swagger).

clickhouse_db_user.label: ClickHouse database username and password
clickhouse_db_user.setup: Create or use a ClickHouse user for the target service/instance and supply its username and password when connecting over the HTTP interface or to the MCP server's backend ClickHouse connection. For Cloud, start from your service in the [ClickHouse Cloud console](https://console.clickhouse.cloud/). For self-managed ClickHouse, create/manage users under the server's authentication configuration and user management docs linked from [Security and authentication](https://clickhouse.com/docs/security-and-authentication).

mcp_static_bearer.label: Static bearer token for self-hosted ClickHouse MCP server
mcp_static_bearer.setup: For HTTP or SSE deployments of the self-hosted MCP server, generate any secure random token (for example with `uuidgen` or `openssl rand -hex 32`) and set it as `CLICKHOUSE_MCP_AUTH_TOKEN` when starting the server, as documented in the [mcp-clickhouse README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/mcp-clickhouse/main/README.md). Configure your MCP client to send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` to the MCP server.

mechanics.command: configure the stdio MCP server process environment
